settings.text: FOR THOSE WHO ARE BUILT FOR THE BATTLE

At Titan BattleGear, we innovate beyond limits. We protect players so they can play harder, longer, and without fear.

We’re looking for people who see the game — and the world — differently. Titans don’t follow the status quo. They challenge it, disrupt it, and rebuild it stronger.

If you’re ready to fight for innovation, performance, and safety in the game we love — you belong here.

We’re Redefining Hockey Gear
Cut-resistant fabrics. Bold designs. USA manufacturing. We’re changing how the game looks, feels, and plays.

Your Work Saves Lives
Every product we make is designed to protect players from life-changing injuries. This isn’t just apparel — it’s armor.

No Passengers
Every Titan pulls their weight. You’ll own your projects, make real impact, and grow with the brand.

We Build Locally, Think Globally
Proudly producing in the USA and partner facilities we trust, we’re scaling worldwide without sacrificing values.

Passion for Hockey – You love the game, live it, and get why it matters.

Growth Mindset – You push boundaries, learn fast, and adapt faster.

Never Compromise – Safety, performance, and quality are non-negotiable.

• Competitive pay with equity opportunities
  • Real impact from day one
  • Work with elite athletes & innovators
  • Fast-growing startup energy with stability of a long-term mission

settings.heading: Territory Sales Representative – Team & Specialty Sales

settings.text:
Job Title: Territory Sales Representative – Team & Specialty Sales

Territory: Midwest Region

Department: Sales

Employment Type: Full-Time

Job Summary:

Titan BattleGear is looking for a motivated and energetic Territory Sales Representative to lead Team and Specialty sales efforts throughout the Midwest. You'll work with youth and adult hockey teams, retailers, and pro shops to sell high performance hockey apparel and gear. Responsibilities include prospecting, cold calling, setting appointments with prospects, presenting programs and meeting a sales quota. 

 

Key Responsibilities:

  • Manage relationships with youth, high school, junior, college, and pro hockey teams
  • Sell customized teamwear tailored to each organization’s needs
  • Lead team fittings, presentations, and ordering consultations
  • Attend tournaments, showcases, and hockey events to network and generate leads
  • Develop new business with pro shops, independent retailers, and organizations
  • Grow and maintain a pipeline of leads and opportunities across the Midwest
  • Travel regularly within the region to maintain in-person client relationships and attend key events
  • Accurately track sales activity, customer interactions, and order progress in CRM tools
  • Meet or exceed monthly and quarterly sales targets

 

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Sports Management, or a related field (preferred)
  • 1–2 years of experience in sales, customer service, or the team sports industry
  • Passion for hockey and a strong understanding of the team and retail landscape
  • Strong interpersonal, organizational, and communication skills
  • Self-driven and comfortable working independently in a field-based role
  • Willingness to travel throughout the upper Midwest, including nights and weekends
  • Role is ideally located in Minnesota
  • Hunter sales mentality - goal driven and self-motivated

 

What We Offer:

  • Base salary + uncapped commissions
  • Accelerated bonus opportunity
  • Employee Stock Option Plan
  • Opportunity to grow within a dynamic, hockey-focused brand
  • A reasonable estimate of total compensation for this role is $60,000-$100,000 and is a combination of base salary, earned commissions, and bonus opportunities. 

 

About Titan BattleGear

Born out of frustration with unsafe and underperforming hockey equipment, Titan BattleGear was created to challenge the status quo. We built Titan for athletes who demand more innovation, more protection, and more style. With decades of expertise in performance apparel and product design, our team set out to create gear that merges cutting-edge technology with sleek, player-focused design. From superior safety to comfort and performance, every product is designed to empower athletes to conquer their game.

Job Title: Senior Paid Media & Growth Lead

Territory: Remote

Department: Marketing

Employment Type: Full-Time

Read this first

Titan BattleGear is bringing growth marketing and paid media fully in-house. This role is not an experiment or a support function. 

Paid media is Titan’s primary growth lever. This role carries real responsibility, real accountability, and real visibility.

You will be in the numbers every single day. You will make decisions that directly affect revenue, demand, and the future of the brand.

If you want a role where success is abstract, timelines are vague, or “doing your best” is enough – this is not the job for you.

What you will own

You are not “running ads.” You will own Titan’s paid media growth engine.

Strategy & creative leadership

  • Develop the creative strategy behind Titan’s growth marketing – formats, storylines, angles, and why messages work.
  • Turn product truths into high-performing ad concepts.
  • Design structured testing plans (What we’re testing, Why it matters, What success looks like)
  • Partner closely with the design team to iterate, refine, and evolve creative quickly and intelligently.

Hands-on execution

  • Build, launch, and optimize campaigns across: Meta (Facebook / Instagram), Google (Search, Shopping, YouTube)
  • Own audience segmentation: Personas, D2C vs B2B, Prospecting, retargeting, and expansion
  • Set up and launch new channels from scratch: Microsoft/Bing, Experimental platforms (Brave, Reddit, AppLovin, others)
  • Own & upgrade Analytics: Maintain pixel health, conversion tracking, and attribution sanity

Measurement, learning & accountability

  • Review performance daily
  • Make fast, informed decisions based on real data, not vibes.
  • Maintain clear, disciplined logs of: Tests run, Results, Learnings, Next actions
  • Know the difference between: Short-term fluctuation, True signal, Creative fatigue
  • Take ownership when things don’t work and fix them.

Growth mindset

  • Stay obsessively current on Google and Meta platform updates.
  • Constantly look for the next lever: New audiences, creative angles, channels, platform tactics
  • Bring ideas proactively. This role is not reactive.

How you’ll work

  • This is a full-time, remote role.
  • You will work closely with Titan’s leadership.
  • You will review performance frequently and directly.
  • You will be expected to think, execute, measure, and iterate continuously.
  • There is no agency buffer and no one else to hand this off to. You own it.

Who this role is for

  • Full-stack paid media experts who can think strategically and execute at the detail level.
  • Marketers who dream up creative concepts, not just place them on platforms.
  • Marketers who enjoy accountability, visibility, and winning. 
  • Marketers who want to build something that matters. This role directly impacts how fast we grow, how many players we can protect, and how safe we can make hockey. 
  • Marketers who are excited by pressure, ownership, and building a growth engine that actually works.
  • Hockey enthusiasts with a passion for hard work and winning big.

Who this role is not for

  • Marketers who only want to manage campaigns, not own outcomes.
  • Marketers who need heavy process, hand-holding, or constant direction.
  • Marketers who are uncomfortable being measured closely and frequently.
  • Marketers who are looking for a comfortable role.

Territory: Remote

Department: Operations

Employment Type: Full-Time

Who We Are

Titan BattleGear builds the safest, most innovative hockey protective gear on the planet, engineered with cutting-edge materials and designed for athletes who refuse to settle. We challenge the status quo of traditional equipment and push every part of our business to perform at elite levels.

We are seeking a highly skilled Demand, Inventory & Sourcing Lead to lead planning excellence and inventory optimization across our product portfolio (cut-resistant baselayers, neck guards, rinkwear and custom team programs). This role bridges commercial insight, operational rigor and supply chain execution to ensure we deliver the right product, at the right time, in the right quantities.

Reporting to the COO, you will be a key member of the operations leadership team, helping shape supply strategies that support growth, reduce stock risk, strengthen supplier partnerships and elevate customer delight.

What You’ll Own

Demand Planning & Forecasting

  • Build and maintain statistically-driven demand forecasts that align with sales goals, seasonal trends, promotions and new product launches.

  • Partner closely with marketing, sales, ecommerce and wholesale to synthesize demand signals and refine forecasts.

  • Translate forecast insights into actionable supply recommendations to drive profitability and service levels.

Sourcing & Purchasing

  • Own purchasing execution for finished goods and raw materials, translating demand plans into accurate purchase orders while balancing lead times, MOQs, and inventory targets.

  • Work directly with manufacturers and specialty material suppliers to manage supply commitments, monitor performance, and proactively address risks to availability, cost, or quality.

  • Support new product development and cost optimization efforts by identifying sourcing opportunities, improving order economics, and maintaining clean, reliable purchasing data in the ERP (Katana experience a plus).

Inventory Strategy & Optimization

  • Set inventory targets (safety stock, reorder points, lead-time buffers) balancing service, cash flow and risk.

  • Monitor inventory performance metrics (turns, sell-through, excess & obsolescence) and implement continuous improvement plans.

  • Lead SKU rationalization discussions to remove underperformers and focus resources on high-impact lines.

Supplier & Manufacturer Management

  • Work directly with domestic manufacturers, fabric mills (including specialty cut-resistant materials) and overseas suppliers to plan reliable supply flows.

  • Coordinate material requirements (mill lead-times, minimums, batch sizes) and communicate prioritized needs.

  • Facilitate quarterly business reviews with key suppliers to assess performance, capacity, risks and opportunities.

Operations Execution & Cross-Functional Alignment

  • Partner with operations and fulfillment teams to align on production scheduling, inventory placement and fulfillment priorities.

  • Support supply chain risk management (capacity constraints, logistics disruptions, raw material volatility).

  • Lead weekly S&OP cadence with commercial leadership to align demand plans, inventory positions and financial outlook.

What We’re Looking For

Experience & Skills

  • 5+ years in demand planning, inventory management or supply planning roles with ecommerce (DTC) or consumer goods experience.
  • Shopify experience for both OMS, catalog and inventory management.

  • Strong analytical mindset, comfortable with forecasting models, data analytics and inventory optimization tools.

  • ERP experience required, experience with Katana (or similar modern ERP/MRP systems) strongly preferred.

  • Demonstrated ability to work across functions and influence without authority.

  • Supply chain execution experience with multiple manufacturing partners and raw material suppliers.

You Are Someone Who

  • Thrives in a high-growth, mission-driven organization where innovation and performance matter.

  • Sees inventory not just as cost but as strategic leverage for growth and customer experience.

  • Thinks ahead, anticipates risk and takes action to keep supply aligned with market demand.

  • Communicates clearly, owns decisions and elevates the teams around you.

Why Titan BattleGear

  • Join a young, innovative brand redefining safety standards in hockey.

  • Work directly with product, commercial and operations leadership to scale impact.

  • Competitive compensation and performance incentives.

  • Culture rooted in authenticity, performance and belonging.

If you are passionate about getting the right gear into players’ hands at the right time, and want to bring world-class planning discipline to a team that lives for innovation, we want to talk.

Social Media Manager & Content Creator

About Titan BattleGear

Titan BattleGear is the boldest, most disruptive and innovative name in hockey protective gear. We make A9 cut-resistant baselayers and neck guards certified by HECC and approved for play by the NHL, the official supplier to USA Hockey and the PWHL. Built with proprietary TITANOTEX material, our patented ArcGuard contoured collar and Stretch ProCurve no-velcro stretch technology are redefining what protection feels like. We are headquartered in Coral Springs, FL and we are on a mission to conquer the hockey world. Together We Conquer.

The Role

Titan BattleGear is looking for a highly creative, hockey-obsessed Social Media Manager & Content Creator to help build one of the fastest-growing brands in hockey.

This role is heavily focused on short-form content creation, videography, editing, and social storytelling across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, and emerging platforms.

You will help capture the energy of the brand through:

  • Athlete shoots
  • Product storytelling
  • Tournament coverage
  • Retail activations
  • Behind-the-scenes content
  • Viral social concepts
  • Team and ambassador content
  • Founder-led storytelling

We are looking for someone who deeply understands hockey culture and can create content that actually resonates with players, parents, and the next generation of the game.

Ideal candidates are:

  • Current or former hockey players
  • Huge hockey fans immersed in the culture
  • Creators with strong instincts for sports storytelling
  • Self-starters who thrive in fast-moving startup environments

This role can be structured as:

  • Summer internship
  • Part-time role
  • Full-time role for recent graduates

Responsibilities

  • Capture and create daily social media content across multiple platforms
  • Shoot and edit short-form videos, reels, TikToks, interviews, and product content
  • Travel to tournaments, retail activations, events, and team visits when needed
  • Work directly with leadership on campaigns and brand storytelling
  • Develop creative concepts aligned with Titan’s bold and disruptive identity
  • Manage posting schedules, community engagement, and content calendars
  • Coordinate with athletes, ambassadors, influencers, and teams
  • Assist with photography, launches, giveaways, and campaigns
  • Identify social trends, viral formats, and opportunities within sports and hockey culture
  • Help scale Titan into a category-leading hockey media and lifestyle brand

Requirements

  • Strong understanding of hockey culture and language
  • Experience creating content for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube
  • Comfortable shooting video on iPhone, DSLR, mirrorless, or similar equipment
  • Experience with editing platforms such as CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or similar
  • Strong creative instincts for pacing, hooks, transitions, and storytelling
  • Ability to move quickly and execute independently
  • Strong communication and organizational skills
  • Comfortable working in a fast-paced startup environment
  • Bonus: photography, graphic design, motion graphics, or paid social experience

AI & Creative Technology Fluency

We are looking for creators who embrace emerging technology and know how to move fast.

Preferred skills include:

  • AI fluency and comfort using modern AI tools to accelerate creative workflows
  • Experience with platforms such as ChatGPT, Midjourney, Runway, Adobe Firefly, CapCut AI, or similar tools
  • Ability to use AI for:Creative ideationScriptingEditing assistanceThumbnail conceptsSocial copy generationTrend researchWorkflow automationContent optimization
  • Understanding that AI enhances creativity and execution, not replaces originality and taste

What Will Separate You

  • You understand both hockey culture and internet culture
  • You know how to create content that feels native to TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts
  • You move fast without sacrificing quality
  • You are early on trends, tools, and platform shifts
  • You embrace AI and new technology instead of resisting it
  • You think like a creator, not just a marketer
  • You want to help build a category-defining brand, not just manage social posts

Location

FL, CT, NY, MN preferred, but open to exceptional candidates.

Compensation

Compensation will depend on experience and structure of the role:

  • Internship
  • Hourly
  • Salary
  • Potential performance incentives and long-term growth opportunities

Titan BattleGear is an equal opportunity employer. We believe the best teams are built from diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives.

Hybrid or Remote | Full-Time | Equity + Cash Compensation

About Titan BattleGear

Titan BattleGear was born out of frustration with outdated, underperforming hockey equipment. Mass-market brands treated neck protection as an afterthought – uninspired gear that didn’t reflect the intensity of the game. We set out to change that. Our cut-resistant baselayer and neck guards are built on TITANOTEX™ fabric, a patented Stretch ProCurve™ Collar, and ArcGuard™ Technology, carrying an ANSI A9 cut resistance rating – the highest available. We don’t just meet the standard, we set it. We’ve grown to six figures in monthly revenue, we’re accelerating, and this is our first full-time marketing hire.

The Role

The foundation is built. The channels are live, the brand voice is locked, the agencies are in place, and the partnerships are meaningful. What we’ve never had is someone who owns marketing full-time.

That changes with this hire. 

You’ll work directly with the Founder, shape how the most disruptive brand in the game of hockey shows up across every channel, and have real ownership over a marketing function that touches paid media, partnerships with the NHL and USA Hockey, athlete sponsorships, live events, video content, and organic social. There’s no committee. No time wasted debating. You’ll move fast, see your work in the market quickly, and watch it drive real revenue.

If you want a brand worth believing in, a team you can grow with, and a role where your fingerprints are on everything, this is it.

Responsibilities

Channel performance

  • Manage our email and Google Ads agencies – set direction, review performance, hold them to results.
  • Own Meta Ads in-house: strategy, creative direction, and execution. Agency support is a future option as we scale.
  • Maintain a disciplined optimization cadence across all paid and owned channels.

Partnership marketing

  • Activate our partnerships with USA Hockey, the PWHL, the NHL, and additional leagues, ensuring they drive real marketing value, not just brand visibility. 
  • Coordinate with our Athlete Marketing team – a group of contracted hockey and player marketing specialists – to align athlete partnerships, NIL deals, Titan ambassadors, and sponsorships with broader campaign strategy.

Events

  • Own end-to-end execution for tournament sponsorships, trade shows, and industry events – from logistics to product sales to geo-targeted ads around an event.
  • Track event performance and use it to inform future sponsorship investment.

Content and video

  • Lead our customer story video series – identifying the right players and moments, coordinating production and editing with our video agency, and distributing across channels. 
  • Brief and direct creative partners and designers on content needs. You own the strategy and the narrative.

Social media

  • Own Titan’s organic social presence across platforms.
  • Build and maintain a content calendar that integrates product, partnerships, culture, and customer stories into a consistent brand voice.

What we’re looking for

  • 4-8 years in marketing with meaningful DTC and consumer brand experience.
  • Shopify experience required – you understand how the platform drives marketing performance
  • Hands-on Meta Ads experience required – strategy, creative direction, campaign build, and optimization. You’re not overseeing it, you’re running it.
  • Google Ads experience required – comfortable reviewing performance, directing strategy, and holding an agency accountable to results. If we should part ways with the agency, you will be expected to take over day-to-day management.
  • Email marketing experience required – You’ve worked in Klaviyo, understand flows and campaigns, can build an email calendar, understand segmentation and loyalty, and know what good looks like. 
  • Demonstrated ability to manage and direct agency partners effectively.
  • Strong written communication skills. You write like a human being – direct, confident, and without filler.
  • High organizational discipline and comfort operating in a lean, contractor-heavy environment with limited hand-holding. 
  • Hockey or sports familiarity is a genuine advantage.
  • Experience working directly with a Founder in a fast-moving environment. 

Compensation

  • Total Comp: $90,000-100,000; comp mix is cash, equity, and bonus
  • Performance bonus: Eligible for an annual performance bonus tied to marketing-attributed revenue growth
  • Equity: Titan BattleGear is in an active growth phase and this role includes an ownership stake in what we’re building. Titan is growing north of 350% growth YoY, so the equity compensation is where the real money is made. You will have the ability to significantly impact this portion of the compensation with your work. Details discussed during the offer process.
  • Full-time, salaried
  • Remote
  • Reports to Founder/CEO

Coral Springs, FL | Full-Time | Equity + Cash Compensation

About Titan BattleGear

Titan BattleGear is the boldest, most disruptive and innovative name in hockey protective gear. We make A9 cut-resistant baselayers and neck guards certified by HECC and approved for play by the NHL, the official supplier to USA Hockey and the PWHL. Built with proprietary TITANOTEX material, our patented ArcGuard contoured collar and Stretch ProCurve no-velcro stretch technology are redefining what protection feels like. We are headquartered in Coral Springs, FL and we are on a mission to conquer the hockey world. Together We Conquer.

The Role

We do not hire people to maintain the status quo. We hire people to help us break it.

As our Operations Manager, you will work directly alongside our Founder and CEO, owning the operational backbone of a fast-moving, category-defining brand: production planning, purchasing, supply chain, and project execution. You will keep our supply chain tight, our projects moving, and our team locked in on outcomes that matter.

This is not a task-management role. This is a build-and-execute role for someone who is obsessed with getting things done, driven by data, and excited to be part of something genuinely disruptive. If you are sharp, analytically minded, and relentlessly organized, this role will teach you more in 12 months than most people learn in five years.

What You Will Do

Production Planning

  • Translate sales forecasts into production schedules across our U.S.-based manufacturing network
  • Monitor inventory levels in Katana MRP and flag reorder points before they become problems
  • Coordinate with manufacturing partners to manage lead times, quantities, and quality checkpoints
  • Keep finished goods, WIP, and raw material data clean, current, and actionable

Supply Chain & Purchasing

  • Own the purchase order process from creation through receipt and reconciliation
  • Manage vendor relationships and track costs, lead times, and delivery performance
  • Work closely with our CPO to align purchasing with product roadmap and production needs
  • Reconcile purchasing data across Katana, QuickBooks, and supplier records

Project Management

  • Own and drive key initiatives across product, operations, and fulfillment in Asana
  • Build and maintain project timelines, surface blockers early, and keep the team accountable
  • Document processes and build repeatable, scalable workflows as we grow
  • Identify opportunities to automate manual tasks and push team efficiency forward using AI tools

CEO Shadow

  • Sit in on strategic meetings and help drive execution across all areas of the business
  • Get exposure to fundraising, partnerships, retail, and brand building
  • Grow into broader leadership as we scale

What We Are Looking For

We are not looking for someone to fill a seat. We are looking for someone who wants to help build something that has never been done before in this sport.

Must Haves

  • 1 to 3 years of experience in operations, supply chain, purchasing, or project management, or a recent graduate with strong internship experience and exceptional drive
  • Obsessive attention to detail: you catch what others miss and you never let the ball drop
  • Strong data and analytical skills, comfortable building reports, tracking KPIs, and turning numbers into decisions
  • Familiarity with BI tools, dashboards, or data visualization (even if self-taught)
  • A genuine interest in AI and automation: you actively look for ways to work smarter, not just harder
  • Tech-savvy and fast to learn new platforms. Our stack includes Shopify, Katana MRP, QuickBooks, ShipStation, Asana, Slack, and Google Workspace
  • Strong written communication and the ability to keep cross-functional teams aligned and moving

Nice to Haves

  • Experience with any part of our tech stack
  • Background in consumer products, apparel, or sporting goods
  • Passion for hockey or team sports

Location

  • Must be local to South Florida. This is a full-time, in-person role in Coral Springs, FL

Compensation

  • Competitive base salary commensurate with experience
  • Equity stake in Titan BattleGear: get in early and share in the upside
  • Direct mentorship from the Founder and CEO
  • A front-row seat at the most exciting brand in hockey

Titan BattleGear is an equal opportunity employer. We believe the best teams are built from diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives.
